scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: every documented v4 endpoint is now covered_by a stream/write or excluded with a specific reason. 6 read streams (waivers, checkins, templates, published_keys, user_info, plus new settings) and 4 write actions (set_webhook_config, resend_webhook, send_sms, prefill_template). Per-waiver/per-template sub-resource detail endpoints (files/photos/signatures/single-waiver/single-template) are excluded as binary_payload/duplicate_of; the search and dynamic-template signing-session endpoints are excluded as out_of_scope (async job-poll / multi-step session flows, not a syncable object list nor a single-shot mutation); webhook-queue consumer endpoints are excluded as out_of_scope (message-queue consume/delete pattern, not a repeatable read).

excluded.reason: single-waiver detail; identical per-item record shape to the waivers list stream's own records, already covered by that stream
returns uploaded file attachments (base64/binary content), not tabular sync data
returns participant photo attachments (base64/binary content), not tabular sync data
returns signature image data (base64/binary content), not tabular sync data
single-template detail; identical per-item record shape to the templates list stream's own records, already covered by that stream
account identity probe used only by Check, not a syncable object stream
webhook configuration read; the same single-object account setting this bundle's set_webhook_config write mutates, no independent sync value as a standalone stream
webhook-queue message-count summary; an operational polling endpoint for the queue-delivery integration pattern, not sync data
retrieves one queued webhook message off the account queue for consume-and-delete processing; a message-queue consumer pattern, not a repeatable list read
retrieves one queued webhook message off a per-template queue for consume-and-delete processing; a message-queue consumer pattern, not a repeatable list read
deletes a consumed message off the account webhook queue; only meaningful paired with the queue GET above, which is itself out of scope
deletes a consumed message off a per-template webhook queue; only meaningful paired with the queue GET above, which is itself out of scope
creates a short-lived dynamic waiver-template signing session (returns a session token for embedding), not a durable record mutation the engine's request/response write model represents
advances a multi-step dynamic-template signing transaction opened by /v4/dynamic/templates, which is itself out of scope

starts an async server-side search job (returns a search token polled via /v4/search/{token}/results); the declarative dialect's stream model has no job-submit-then-poll read shape, and the documented streams already cover the same waivers/checkins data via direct filterable list reads
polls results for the async search job started at /v4/search, which is itself out of scope
